title: Dried Tunisian Pinto Beans Tagine
description: A rich, low-calorie one-pot dish from Tunisia, featuring pinto beans, preserved lemons, and a tangy manteca emulsion.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large clay or ceramic pot over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and cook until translucent, about 8 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Add the diced preserved lemon and cook for 1 minute.
Add the ground cumin, coriander, red pepper flakes, and salt. Cook for 1 minute.
Add the sorted and rinsed pinto beans, and 2 cups of water to the pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 1 hour.
After 1 hour, use an immersion blender to puree the tagine until slightly smooth.
Stir in the chopped fresh parsley and cook for an additional 10 minutes.
To make the manteca emulsion, whisk together 1/2 cup of water and 2 tablespoons of olive oil in a small bowl.
Gradually pour the manteca emulsion into the tagine, whisking constantly.
Continue to simmer the tagine for an additional 10-15 minutes, or until the emulsion has thickened and the tagine has reached the desired consistency.
